question: Ask the user to enter their full name (which may include spaces). Read the entire line into an `std::string`. Then, print the full name back to the user and also display the total number of characters in the name.

test_cases.expected:
Please enter your full name: Your full name is: John Doe
Total characters: 8

Please enter your full name: Your full name is: Alice Wonderland
Total characters: 16

Please enter your full name: Your full name is: SingleWord
Total characters: 10

Please enter your full name: Your full name is:  A Name With Leading Space
Total characters: 24

Please enter your full name: Your full name is: 
Total characters: 0

errors: Reading input character by character using `std::cin.get()` and manually building the string is less efficient and readable than using `std::getline(std::cin, fullName)`.
Manually iterating through the string with a `for` loop to count characters is inefficient and less readable compared to using `fullName.length()` or `fullName.size()`.
Includes an unnecessary header `<vector>`, which adds to code clutter.
